features.summary: Amazon reported Q4 2024 revenue of $187.8 billion, up 10% year-over-year, with operating income of $21.2 billion, up 61%. AWS grew 19% to a $115 billion annualized run rate, while advertising revenue reached $17.3 billion, up 18%. The company delivered over 9 billion units same or next day globally and expanded same-day delivery sites by 60%. Amazon reduced global cost-to-serve per unit for the second consecutive year while improving delivery speed and safety.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q1 2025 revenue between $151-155.5 billion with operating income of $14-18 billion. Capital investments of approximately $26.3 billion quarterly run rate will continue in 2025, primarily for AI infrastructure supporting AWS and fulfillment network expansion. The company anticipates supply constraints in AI capacity to ease in the second half of 2025, potentially enabling faster AWS growth. Amazon plans further cost reductions through improved inventory placement, same-day delivery expansion, and increased robotics automation.
features.risks: Foreign exchange headwinds created $700 million more impact than anticipated in Q4, with $2.1 billion expected headwind in Q1. Supply chain constraints including chip availability, power limitations, and component shortages are moderating AWS growth potential. The company faces margin pressure from heavy AI investments in the near term. Useful life changes for servers and network equipment will decrease 2025 operating income by approximately $700 million due to accelerated technology development in AI.
features.key_drivers: AI services are driving triple-digit growth within AWS, with Amazon launching Nova frontier models and expanding Bedrock capabilities. The company's custom Trainium 2 chips offer 30-40% better price performance than GPU alternatives, attracting major customers like Anthropic. Prime membership growth accelerated due to expanded benefits including fuel discounts and faster delivery. Robotics and automation investments in fulfillment centers are showing significant productivity improvements, with plans to expand beyond the successful Shreveport facility implementation.
